message: what is 2+2?
say hello
use the calculate tool to compute (123 + 456) * 7
Please remember this preference: I prefer Base chain over Ethereum mainnet for testing.
Use recall_memory to look up which chain I prefer.
What skills are available? Use list_skills.
Use the playwright MCP to navigate to https://example.com and tell me the page title.
use playwright to open https://etherscan.io/tx/0x5798869a235d237ff3d8a97079c68debb22fa8051e92ae25632a92de700557a4 and summarise the tx
Use eth_getBalance to check the ETH balance of 0x6b175474e89094c44da98b954eedeac495271d0f on ethereum.

Use fetch_url on https://twitter.com/jack/status/20 and tell me exactly what the tweet says.
Use fetch_url on https://example.com and tell me the title of the page.

Write a 50000-word essay on the history of computing, including detailed sections on each decade from 1940 to 2020.

description: Two-step composition: web_search returns sources → agent picks one → fetch_url reads it
message: Find one recent article about Pendle Finance using web_search, then use fetch_url on the most relevant result URL to read it. Tell me the article's title and a 2-sentence summary.

description: Three-step: get contract bytecode via eth_getCode → search to identify the contract → return identity
message: Use eth_getCode to fetch the bytecode at 0xC36442b4a4522E871399CD717aBDD847Ab11FE88 on ethereum. Then use web_search to find what protocol this address belongs to. Tell me the protocol name and what this contract does.

description: Memory recall → contextual search: agent must use a stored preference to shape the search
message: First save_memory of type 'preference' with text 'I am only interested in Layer-2 networks, especially Base and Arbitrum'. Then use web_search to find the latest news about scaling solutions, applying my preference. Summarize what you found in 3 bullet points.

description: Four-step: fetch tweet → extract claim → search to verify → report verdict
message: Use fetch_url on https://twitter.com/jack/status/20 to read what the tweet says. Then use web_search to find context about who the author is and when this tweet was posted. Tell me: 1) what the tweet says verbatim, 2) who the author is, 3) why this tweet is historically notable.

description: Five-step deep-dive: on-chain data → search for context → optionally fetch source → synthesize verdict
message: Do a deep dive on this token: 0x6982508145454ce325ddbe47a25d4ec3d2311933 on ethereum. Use combined_token_analysis to get the on-chain data, then use web_search to find recent sentiment/news about it. Tell me: 1) the token name and current price, 2) liquidity health (is it tradeable?), 3) the top 1 risk you'd flag based on news. Be concise — 4 bullet points max.
